result:
Subject: About yesterday's payments outage

Dear Customer,

Yesterday, our payments service was unavailable for approximately four hours, and some of your transactions failed as a result. We are deeply sorry. Whether you were trying to complete a purchase, settle an invoice, or run payroll, the disruption had real consequences, and the responsibility for it sits with us.

The root cause was a misconfigured cache invalidation rule in our payments layer. Under a traffic spike, the rule caused stale data to be served to downstream systems, which in turn rejected a subset of otherwise valid transactions. Once we identified the faulty rule, we rolled it back and restored full service. No customer funds were lost or misdirected, and no sensitive data was exposed.

To prevent a recurrence, we are tightening how we ship changes to the payments path. Going forward, configuration updates of this kind will be released through canary rollouts, so a small slice of traffic validates the change before it reaches everyone. We are also expanding chaos testing in our staging environment to simulate traffic spikes and failure modes like this one, so we catch these issues before they ever touch production.

As a gesture of goodwill and an acknowledgment that an apology alone is not enough, we will be applying a credit to the account of every customer whose transactions were impacted during the outage window. You do not need to take any action; the credit will appear on your next statement. If you have questions or believe a transaction was affected but not captured, please reach out to our support team and we will make it right.

Thank you for your patience and for your continued trust. We know that trust is earned, and we intend to earn it back.

Sincerely,
The Payments Team
